other_observers: none; I am the orginal finder
specific_location: DeWeese Reservoir SWA. Along Grape Creek about 150 yards downstream of the dam.
description: The bird was comparable in size and shape to other warblers nearby, i.e. Wilson and Yellow-rumped. The upper-parts (back, head and upper tail) were blue-gray. The under-parts (throat, breast and belly) were bright yellow. There was a faint necklace of dark streaks across the breast. It had a complete white eye-ring. There was yellow on the lores. No wing-bars. NOTE: I did not spend any time making detailed observations of this bird. After I recognized that it was a CAWA, I spent the rest of the time attempting to get usable photographs of it.Behaviors: The CAWA was actively feeding in the shrubs and plants along the creek and on the cliff. The photos were taken when the bird was feeding in plants on the cliff.Call: None heardPlumage: adult
photographer: Photographer: Rich Miller, Rich Miller, Rich Miller
